pi_items.source: Code inspection: setup_task.sh line 9 and export_result.sh line 6 both define this path consistently
This is a validation threshold to ensure the mesh is non-degenerate. No contradictory evidence found. Typical mandible STL exports have >10,000 triangles.

Anatomical verification via web search. Anterior mandible height is ~65-67mm (LAFH). Cranium alone is ~120-125mm. Total anterior facial height (nasion-menton) is ~108-114mm. Total craniofacial height is ~180-220mm. The 120mm threshold appropriately distinguishes an isolated mandible from the full skull (~200mm). Verifier comment confirms this intent.

Literature search found edentulous mandible volume of approximately 49,213 mm³ (within the threshold range). Typical adult mandible volume is 30,000-50,000 mm³. The 20,000 mm³ lower bound allows for smaller specimens or incomplete segmentation.

Anatomical bounds verification. Typical adult mandible volume is 30,000-50,000 mm³. The 200,000 mm³ upper bound is appropriate to detect over-segmentation (inclusion of other skull bones). This value is consistent with quality control for a mandible-only extraction task.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the CT Cranium (0051) DICOM dataset from the public InVesalius v3.0 release (25.5 MB, 8,844 downloads). The metadata thresholds are anatomically grounded: max_z_span_mm=120.0 distinguishes the isolated mandible (~65-67mm vertical height) from the full craniofacial structure (~180-220mm); min/max volume bounds of 20,000-200,000 mm³ constrain the output to realistic mandible geometry (typical ~30,000-50,000 mm³). The output file format is binary STL at /home/ga/Documents/mandible.stl. All verifiable claims are consistent with anatomical standards and medical imaging practices.
